summary: Web Client - 'Child' Keywords within a Cascading Dataset have no selectable values when the 'Parent' Keyword--which is a 'Child' of the Root value--is hidden.
description: [ Summary / Description ] In the Web Client, 'Child' keyword within a Cascading Data Set contains no values when its 'Parent' keyword--which is a 'Child' of the Root keyword--is configured as a 'Hidden' keyword. [ Business Impact ] Customer is forced to make a keyword they would prefer to have 'Hidden' visible for this process to work. They have had this process in place for some time, which works in 18, however now it is preventing an upgrade.[ Background ] Testing has confirmed that this issue does not occur in the Thick Client. [ Actual Behaviour vs. Desired Behaviour ] Actual behaviour: In the Web Client, 'Parent' keyword within a Cascading Dataset--which is a 'Child' of the Root keyword--must be set to visible for its 'Child' keyword to display selectable values in the Web Client. Desired behaviour: In the Web Client, 'Child' keyword within a Cascading Data Set will display selectable values regardless of whether its 'Parent' keyword---which is a 'Child' of the Root keyword--is configured as 'Hidden' or not. [ Latest Version/Build Tested ] 25.1[ Workarounds ] Do not configure the 'Parent' keyword--which is a 'Child' of the Root keyword--as a 'Hidden' keyword on the Document Type.

summary: The following error is thrown when the 'Replace Keyword' action is executed with an intentionally null value for a keyword that is apart of a Single Keyword Group: ORA-00001: unique constraint violated
description: [ Template ]Technical Support - Technical Specialist - Defect RFA [OnBase][ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Workflow[ Summary / Description ]The following error is thrown when the 'Replace Keyword' action is executed with an intentionally null value for a keyword that is apart of a Single Keyword Group: Hyland.Data.Oracle.DatabaseOperation.OracleDatabaseException: ORA-00001: unique constraint (HSI.KEYGROUPDATA1705_2) violated ---> Oracle.ManagedDataAccess.Client.OracleException: ORA-00001: unique constraint (HSI.KEYGROUPDATA1705_2) violatedThe issue also happens within SQL[ Business Impact ]Documents fail processing if imported without a SIKG1 value, causing stoppage of the workflow and requires reprocessing of affected documents. For the business case there is an eform in which a property is pulled but is intentionally left null/blank which causes the issue (Set up within the Workflow lifecycle as 'CCO test' timer.[ Actual Behavior vs. Desired Behavior ]Actual Behavior:An error is thrown and documents are not able to be processed when the 'Replace keyword' action is intentionally null and it appears that there is not a check made within the database to see if the value is already within the tableDesired Behavior:To allow null values for the 'Replace Keyword' action without error[ Customer Pain ]2[ Defect Classification ]Usability[ Reported Version/Build ]21.1.5.1000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]24.1.0.6[ Replicated ]Yes[ Location Replicated ]ORASCREP

summary: Re-indexing works within the Web Client but fails within Unity Client when different Hidden and Read Only Settings are set on the Keywords
description:
[ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Web Client/Server[ Summary / Description ]When indexing from one Document Type to another with all the same Keywords on it but one of the Keywords on the first Document Type has "read only" while the second Document Type has "read only" and "hidden" checked, re-indexing between the two fails in the Unity Client, but works in the Web Client.Error Listed in Diagnostic Console:System.InvalidOperationException: An unauthorized attempt to modify or create a hidden and/or read-only keyword has been made. This user session does not have the required Access Restricted Keyword privilege.at Hyland.Core.HashedKeywordDataCache.ValidateRestrictedKeywordData(String uuid, DocumentType documentType, KeywordRecordList receivedRecords, Int64 documentID, TrackedKeywordData& trackedKeywordData)at Hyland.Canvas.Services.UploadServiceClass.ReindexDocument()[ Business Impact ]Lack of consistency between the re-indexes of Unity and Web which the customer leverages both[ Background ]-This appears to be a known issue per the summary of it on the following confluence page:https://hyland.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TechnicalSupport/pages/78775090/Restricted+Keywords+-+Jira+Cards-It is noted that the documentation states the following:"When you configure a Keyword Type as read-only on one Document Type, it is considered a best practice to configure that Keyword Type to be read-only on all Document Types that use that Keyword Type."-The following is also noted in the MRG:"CAUTION: Do not give a Keyword Type both this option and RO - Read only unless the user has Access Restricted Keywords privilege."https://support.hyland.com/r/OnBase/System-Administration-On-Premises/Foundation-25.1/On-Premises-System-Administration/Configuration-Module/Document-Types-and-Document-Type-Groups/Configuring-Document-Type-Keywords/Keyword-Options-There is an ongoing project "
https://hyland.atlassian.net/browse/PORTF-878
" that this may be tied to, but not sure if this particular behavior is covered in the multiple ongoing Defects[ Expected Behavior ]The behavior between the re-index of the Unity and the Web Client should be the same, even if the expectation is that it should throw an error when mismatching Hidden and Read only keywords[ Customer Pain ]2 (Medium/Low)[ Defect Classification ]Usability[ Reported Version/Build ]24.1.7.1000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]26.1.0.381[ Workarounds ]Match the keywords Read-only and hidden fields to allow for re-indexing in the unity client or assign the "Access Restricted Keywords" keywords privilege.This will allow it to work in the unity client but is the expectation that it should fail between web and unity under these circumstances or succeed. My guess would be fail and then the issue would be with the web client re-index[ Potentially Related Cases Reviewed - Support Case, RFAs, TSKB, Jira ]The following page lists details and JIRA's fairly detailed.https://hyland.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TechnicalSupport/pages/78775090/Restricted+Keywords+-+Jira+Cards-There is an ongoing project "
https://hyland.atlassian.net/browse/PORTF-878
" that this may be tied to, but not sure if this particular behavior is covered in the multiple ongoing Defects[ Logs (CDH Location - must be connected to VPN) ]\\onbase.net\shares\CDHS\DataDownload\Westlake\Technical Support\02072007Please Reference ReadMe.txt[ Backport Request ]No[ Replicated ]Yes[ Location Replicated ]ORASCR_Current

summary: When executing a system task "Upload Documents" on a Workview object, the keywords are not carried over from the object to the document import screen within the Web Client.
description:
When executing a system task "Upload Documents" on a Workview object, the keywords are not carried over from the object to the document import screen within the Web Client. This works as excepted in the Unity Client.   [ Template ]Technical Support - Technical Specialist - Defect RFA [OnBase][ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Workflow/WorkView[ Summary / Description ]When executing a system task "Upload Documents" on a Workview object, the keywords are not carried over from the object to the document import screen within the Web Client. This works as excepted in the Unity Client.[ Business Impact ]Web client users are not able to index documents using this streamlined process[ Background ]  This was originally fixed with the Documents | Upload feature via 
https://hyland.atlassian.net/browse/SBPWC-10776
(
https://hyland.atlassian.net/browse/SBPWC-10776
) (TS ticket 01843438)
  The same behavior is present when using a Workflow System Task to import, but the Documents | Upload feature is not a valid workaround here due to having several rules/actions configured on the system task for imported documents[ Expected Behavior ]Actual Behavior:The KTG is not auto-filling in the Web clientDesired Behavior:The KTG auto-fills in the web client like it does in the Unity Client[ Customer Pain ]3 (Medium)[ Defect Classification ]Usability[ Reported Version/Build ]24.1.34.1000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]26.1.0.379[ Workarounds ]  Use the built in Document Import option but this will not execute any other actions that a system task can perform[ Potentially Related Cases Reviewed - Support Case, RFAs, TSKB, Jira ]https://hyland.atlassian.net/browse/SBPWC-10776[ Logs (CDH Location - must be connected to VPN) ]NA[ Backport Request ]Yes[ Backport Version ]24.1[ Customer Confirmation of Upgrade/Update to Backported Version/Build ]Yes[ Escalating Party ]Vivek Kumar (partner)[ Business Case ]Web client users are not able to index documents using this streamlined process[ Replicated ]Yes[ Location Replicated ]SQLTest[ Configuration Items ]Client Found: Web Client (browser does not matter)Version Found: 24.1.34.1000Version Tested: 26.1.0.379Database: SQLTestWorkview Application: 01843438 KTG Mapping[ Steps to Recreate ]To view the intended behavior, first open the Unity client connected to SQLTEST1\. Launch the Workview application "01843438 KTG Mapping"2\. Use the "All Person Objects" filter to select an object3\. Open the object, select the Task tab, use the task 
01843438 KTG Mapping4\. Notice the keywords are automatically filled due to keyword mapping (MIKG1 = "Name" attribute; MIKG2 = "Type" attribute)To view the defective behavior, open the Web client connected to SQLTEST1\. Launch the Workview application "01843438 KTG Mapping"2\. Use the "All Person Objects" filter to select an object3\. Open the object, use the task on the right-hand side 
01843438 KTG Mapping4\. Notice the keywords are not automatically filled like they were in the Unity client

summary: Data Validation - DateTime format provided from DataValidation JS is not working in Hungary
description:
For Hungarian locale, WorkView’s original dotted address value is getting returned from the database with  this format- 
"YYYY. MM. DD. H:mm:SS"
 , while actual format is being expected by DataValidation JS should be like-  
"YYYY. MM. DD.  HH:mm:SS"
. The second format has one extra white space between date and time part in data validation files which is causing client side JS validations for WV Data-Time attribute to fail in all scenarios.A typical full date and time format would look like this: 
"YYYY. MM. DD. HH:mm:SS"

summary: CLONE - DUMMY for testing No action is required
description:
[ System Type ] OnBase [ Module / Product Line Functionality ]
 Revisions/Renditions/Versions [ Summary / Description ]
 When reindexing a document that with the same keywords of another revisable document, the document being reindexed is purged and providing no audit trail within the document history that his occurs This seems to have changed between OnBase 17 to EP5, In OnBase 17, the document reindexed was not purged which allowed the original document history to be seen and it mentions the Document Handle being reindexed to the revisable document. This information cannot be seen in newer versions due to the original document being purged. [ Business Impact ] 
If a user accidently re-indexes a document [ Actual Behavior vs. Desired Behavior ] Actual: When re-indexing a document to have the same keywords as a revisable document, there is no document history reference the document handle of the original document and it is purged from the system Expected: When the re-indexing is complete and the document is purged, would expect an indication of that Doc Handle A was re-indexed as a revision of Doc Handle B [ Customer Pain ]
 2 [ Defect Classification ]
 Usability [ Reported Version/Build ]
 21.1.20.1000 [ Latest Version/Build Tested ] 
24.1.0.16 [ Replicated ]
 Yes [ Location Replicated ]
 ORASCR[ Workarounds ]
 Using an older version of OnBase allowed users to view history on the document being re-indexed [ Other Logs (CDH Location) ]
 \\
onbase.net
\shares\CDHS\DataDownload\Westlake\Technical Support\01215590-Contains a word document with extensive testing from the Analyst, you can see examples of how OnBase 17 handled the re-indexing and document history

summary: Unity Client: Keyword panel error when document auto-open after import

summary: In Unity Client duplicate keyword's fieldname's is  inconsistent over the builds
description: In UC application, in some of the builds (25.1.5.1000), Duplicate keyword’s fields names are coming with some junk character (Press f6 to ESC). (Here name is a control we use as an automation locator to locate the field in our WPF automation framework).Now the issue is, in some of the build the locator Name is coming as keyword field name as it is, in some builds (mentioned in the ticket), it is coming with some additional junk values.Now to locate this element, we need a generalize locator irrespective of the build, as automation script is failing particularly as it is not able to identify the element as the field value is getting changed.P.S: In web probably the property aria-label-description, is containing this same value which in not changing depends on build.

summary: Unable to update/delete an existing Floating Point Keyword in the Core when using an Oracle database and the number is rounded
description:
[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]  
Keywords - Core[ Summary / Description ]Attempting to update or delete a Floating Point Keyword that rounds up in the client will not be updated/deleted if done from the Core when using an Oracle database.For example:If the value in the Oracle database is 26.05999999, the Client will show it as 26.06, when attempting to delete/update this keyword, the database query in the Core will use 26.06 explicitly. In the Thick Client the query uses 26.06, but performs additional logic to handle the rounding.Core
:delete from hsi.keyitem598 where hsi.keyitem598.itemnum = 28395 and hsi.keyitem598.keyvaluefloat = 26.06Thick
:Delete from hsi.keyitem598 Where itemnum = 28395 and ABS(keyvaluefloat - 26.060000) < 0.000001[ Business Impact ]Users are unable to use the Unity Client to update Keywords. Workflow also does not work to update the Keywords either.[ Expected Behavior ]It is expected that the Core is able to delete/update all Keyword values regardless of their value[ Customer Pain ]
 3 (Medium)[ Defect Classification ]
 Usability[ Reported Version/Build ]
 24.1.7.1000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]
 26.1.0.150[ Backport Request ]
 No[ Replicated ]
 Yes[ Location Replicated ]
 SQL-Interop_Current[ Configuration Items ]Unity or Web pointing to SQL Interop CurrentSQL Developer if you want to see the keyword value[ Steps to Recreate ]Log in to Unity as a member of the 'GRP - All Hyland Employees' groupRetrieve the document that is presentEnsure the Keyword value is something that will be rounded eg: 26.059999NOTE: You need to query the DB for this as in the Client it will be rounded and show as 26.06Attempt to delete/update the keywordSaveNote the document history shows the keyword is deleted but it appears back on the document[ Workarounds ]Use the Thick Client[ Potentially Related Cases Reviewed - Support Case, RFAs, TSKB, Jira ]None found specifically for this

summary: Within the Unity Client if a Keyword value is entered that includes the same characters, in the same order, as the Static characters configured within the Masking of said Keyword, the values are being removed and the Keyword is not being saved.
description: [ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Unity Client[ Summary / Description ]Within the Unity Client if a Keyword value is entered that includes the same characters, in the same order, as the Static characters configured within the Masking of said Keyword, the values are being removed and the Keyword is not being saved.This only happens with "Extract Mask Characters" is enabled for the Keyword Type and does not happen when attempting this in the OnBase Client.[ Business Impact ]Users are unable to use "Extract Mask Characters" when the static characters are matching the values after the static characters, as no keyword will be saved.[ Expected Behavior ]The Masking should allow for the characters after the static characters to be saved and stored within OnBase.[ Customer Pain ]1 (Low)[ Defect Classification ]Stability[ Reported Version/Build ]21.1.20.1000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]24.1.0.47[ Replicated ]Yes[ Location Replicated ]ORASCR_Current[ Workarounds ]Customer workaround add a dash between the static characters or add a space to the beginning of the masking.[ Potentially Related Cases Reviewed - Support Case, RFAs, TSKB, Jira ]Possibly related - CSFMD-2314[ Logs (CDH Location) ]Recording of the issue: (\\onbase.net\shares\CDHS\DataDownload\Lincoln\Technical Support\01883488\Recording of the issue captured 2-12-25)[ Backport Request ]No

summary: Reverse Autofill in Web Client Utilizing Special Characters Will Return No Results
description:
Within the Unity Client, a reverse autofill with a special character, such as & returns results. However, in the Web Client we get 'No records found'.   [ Template ]Technical Support - Defect RFA [OnBase][ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Web Client/Server[ Reviewed By ]Robert Miller[ Summary / Description ]When utilizing reverse AFKS in the web client, if the AFKS has a special character like a '&' it will return a message of 'No records found'. Even if there are confirmed keysets within the AFKS with the exact match. A wildcard can be used in place of it and it will work.In the Unity client utilizing the special character '&' will yield the results without error.[ Business Impact ]Users utilizing the web client may not be able to fully utilize the reverse AFKS functionality[ Background ]Utilizing wildcard will return results but cannot use special characters and return the expected resultsFound SCR 217295 that seems to be related to what we are seeing[ Expected Behavior ]Expected behavior is for the reverse AFKS to be able to find the correct AFKS within the web client when utilizing special characters[ Customer Pain ]3 (Medium)[ Defect Classification ]Usability[ Reported Version/Build ]23.1.21.10000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]26.1.0.47[ Replicated ]Yes[ Location Replicated ]Recreated in the ORASCR_Current database[ Configuration Items ]  web client
  an AFKS that has at least one value that contains a '&' - 01873435 reverse AFKS web
  a reverse AFKS of the above AFKS that has a searchable keyword value that contains the special character - 01873435 Reverse AFKS web[ Steps to Recreate ]1.  using latest version of web client log in


2.  go to import document


3.  select document type 01873435 - Reverse AFKS web


4.  Select 'lookup'


5.  enter value 'A&A' in the name keyword select find


6.  note error 'No matching records were found'


7.  Note that you can find the A&A keyword value associated with that AFKS by confirming it in thick client under ADMIN > Autofill Keyword Sets > Management > Selecting 01873435 - Reverse AFKS web and view all


8.  Can run the same test in Unity and it will work using the 'A&A' value  *[ Workarounds ]*Utilizing wildcard '*' instead of the special characters[ Potentially Related Cases Reviewed - Support Case, RFAs, TSKB, Jira ]SCR 217295[ Hyland Diagnostics Logs ]N/A[ Event Viewer Logs ]N/A[ Other Logs (CDH Location) ]N/A[ Backport Request ]No

summary: Keyword Data Set Import box doesn't respect the filename field.
description: [ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Configuration[ Summary / Description ]When browsing to a file for a "Keyword Data Set" Import and then changing the file in the textbox field and clicking Import, the values from the original selected file are imported instead of the listed file name[ Business Impact ]None[ Expected Behavior ]When a user copies or modifies the Import path manually (not using Browse), that path isn't respected for the import.[ Customer Pain ]1 (Low)[ Backport Request ]No

summary: Some configuration automatically adds non-medical docs to hsi.mrorphandocs
description: [ Summary / Description ]When documents are brought into OnBase via a Thick client scheduled sweep, and a medical default doc type is selected when pre-index is configured, the imported document also creates a record in hsi.mrorphandocs. (NOTE: Medical settings chart attachment is set to "On Commit")When the document is indexed in batch processing in the Unity Client to a non-medical doc type, the record in hsi.mrorphandocs is not removed. However if the document is worked in batch processing in the Thick Client and indexed to a non-medical doc type, the record in hsi.mrorphandocs is removed successfully.[ Business Impact ]Customer's hsi.mroprhandocs is filling up with non-medical documents as they are not being removed upon index/commit of the batch when done via the Unity Client.[ Background ]These documents can be corrected manually by re-indexing the document once committed, but only through the thick client re-index action.Issue can occur both with a manual sweep into the scan queue and a scheduled.A re-index of the document in the thick client to the medical doc type and then back to the non-medical doc type will resolve it.[ Expected Behavior ]These documents should not be evaluated on sweep and end up in hsi.mroprhandocs when brought into a scan queue with the default doc type set to a medical doc type (no keyword data).[ Customer Pain ]1 (Low)[ Reported Version/Build ]21.1.41.1000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]26.1.0.60[ Workarounds ]In the thick client once committed, index to a medical doc type and then back to the non-medical doc type. [ Backport Request ]No

summary: Web Client doesn't respect keyword mask when there is an external data set involved.
description:
[ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Web Client/Server[ Summary / Description ]Web Client doesn't respect keyword mask when there is an external data set involved.[ Business Impact ]Using 0 is supposed to generate a number as part of their configuration, but instead it won't let them index the document with the 0 as it does in the Unity Client.[ Actual Behavior vs. Desired Behavior ]Actual Behavior: When attempting to index a value of "0" on the keyword with this configuration, the Web Client throws an error.
Desired Behavior: The Web Client should work like the Unity Client does.[ Customer Pain ]1 (Low)[ Backport Request ]No

summary: Migrating Keyword Groups is considerably slower than Single Keywords
description:
Fiserv is reporting a poor performance challenge when migrating unencrypted keywords in a keyword group to Alpha Encrypted Keywords. They are stating it is 10x slower when in the Keyword Group versus when not. This makes migrations to use Encrypted Alpha Keywords difficult to impossible for customers with large quantities of keywords in keyword groups.The support issue is 
01692571 | Case | Salesforce
.Customer has not provided any specific version from where the software has become significantly slower.

summary: Custom Validation scripts on the web client are giving browser error: 'because its MIME type ('text/html') is not executable, and strict MIME type checking is enabled.'
description: When using Custom Validation for a Keyword during import in the Web Client the browser is unable to execute the script unless it is assigned to both the application and page sections of the web.config Following error within dev tools: ...because its MIME type ('text/html') is not executable, and strict MIME type checking is enabled.   [ Template ]Technical Support - Technical Specialist - Defect RFA [OnBase][ System Type ]OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]Web Client/Server[ Summary / Description ]When using Custom Validation for a Keyword during import in the Web Client the browser is unable to execute the script unless it is assigned to both the application and page sections of the web.configFollowing error within dev tools: ...because its MIME type ('text/html') is not executable, and strict MIME type checking is enabled.[ Business Impact ]Customer is unable to validate Keyword values during importWhen assigning the script to both the application and page, the script is ran but for every call to a keyword panel (import and document retrieval for example)[ Actual Behavior vs. Desired Behavior ]Actual Behavior:Keyword is not validated, error in browser console states '...because its MIME type ('text/html') is not executable, and strict MIME type checking is enabled.'Desired Behavior:Keyword Validation at the page level[ Customer Pain ]2 (Medium/Low)[ Defect Classification ]Usability[ Reported Version/Build ]22.1.20.1000[ Latest Version/Build Tested ]25.1.0.225[ Replicated ]Yes[ Location Replicated ]orascr_current[ Workarounds ]  Can add in the configuration into the Application level, but this will not work for them as the script will attempt to validate everywhere the keyword panel is used[ Potentially Related Cases Reviewed - Support Case, RFAs, TSKB, Jira ]https://hyland.atlassian.net/browse/CSFMD-1569[ Backport Request ]No

summary: Web Client Document Import doesn't preserve Keyword values when changing to another Document in Document Queue
description: Found during regression testing of Test Case #77165: 'Workstation Options - Document Import' for Test Plan #12513 (onbase://wv/object/?classid=12260&objectid=48451678&Sig=PU4y3Bes3Xm6IOV0eysdg34d6Zs4gMy0nYCvB9du9iw~) during the last test, #807078.This is with the “Clear Document Type After Import” web client preference disabled. Expected behavior is that the previous Keyword Values remain for the next document after each Import, but currently moving on to the next document in the queue clears the keyword values in the keyword panel.

summary: The message ”There are no keywords available for this document type.” can be erroneously displayed when the "UseNewKeywordDataCacheImplementation" feature flag is enabled

summary: Re-indexing a document using keywords that belong to an existing document and selecting "Save as Revision" in the existing document revision dialog results in errors shown in Diagnostics Console
description:
Re-indexing a document using keywords that belong to an existing document and selecting "Save as Revision" in the existing document revision dialog results in errors shown in Diagnostics Console.  I have found that these errors can also occur when using the “Append Revision” and “Save as Rendition” options.  Below shows the errors displayed in Diagnostics Console (no further info on stacktrace information in Event Viewer):The second exception appears to be a generic invalid webresource request http exception, but here’s the stacktrace anyways:


System.Web.HttpException (0x80004005): This is an invalid webresource request.


   at System.Web.Handlers.AssemblyResourceLoader.System.Web.IHttpHandler.ProcessRequest(HttpContext context)


   at System.Web.HttpApplication.CallHandlerExecutionStep.System.Web.HttpApplication.IExecutionStep.Execute()


   at System.Web.HttpApplication.ExecuteStepImpl(IExecutionStep step)


   at System.Web.HttpApplication.ExecuteStep(IExecutionStep step, Boolean& completedSynchronously)

description:
[ Description of Issue ]
  Message: 'Document is no longer checked out. A local copy containing your changes can be recovered.' is displayed when viewing “My Checkouts” after another OnBase user on the same workstation as checks out a document[ Available Workarounds ]Log in from another workstation

summary: Losing a MIKG instance when using a No Access Restricted Keywords User to re-index into a Document Type with HID Keywords
description:
Re-indexing a Document with multiple MIKG Keyword instances some of which have a Default value associated into a Document Type that has these Keywords marked as hidden will result in MIKG instances being removed.This is the Document’s state before Re-Indexing:In the Re-Index Dialog we switch to the Document Type with HID Keywords (notice two MIKG instances are here both Blank)After clicking Re-Index we can see that the two MIKG’s got merged into one

And when viewing the document with a user that has Access Restricted Keywords permissions we see that one of the MIKG instances is gone

This has only happened when the MIKG instances both had blank values in the first Keyword and only the second Keyword was different which was also marked as hidden.

summary: Backport 22.1 - Merging an existing value into a masked keyword dataset results in an ODBC error
description:
When an end user attempts to merge an existing value into a dataset for a masked keyword an ODBC error is displayed. Giving them the option to Abort, Retry or Ignore.


Aborting will close out of the client, retrying will constantly prompt the same ODBC error, ignoring will add a 2nd instance of the masked keyword to the dataset. After re-opening the dialog the 2nd instance is removed.This was found in 21.1.4.23 and recreated in 22.1.0.99The ODBC Error will occur when importing values from the text box in the dialog, or when selecting import from file.Occurs when using ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server, ODBC Driver 13 for SQL Server, SQL Server Native Client 11.0

summary: Encrypted AutoFill Keyword Sets will not expand in the Core dependent on maxlength values
description:
Encrypted AutoFill Keyword Sets will not expand in the Core if the primary key is an encrypted keyword with a maximum length setting of less than the hash for the primary



Verified this issue exists regardless of using the 32 or 64 bit application server.
Verified this issue exists regardless of using remoting or soap
Verified Reverse Keysets will also not return any results in the Core.

summary: Keyword panel is cleared after performing an Import when multiple files are dragged in for Import
description: With the Web Client preference “Clear keywords after import” disabled, importing multiple documents still clears the keywords between document imports.

summary: Importing with the following Workstation Options enabled "Clear Keywords After Import" and "Do Not Close Dialog After Import" is clearing default values
description:
When both “Do Not Close Dialog After Import” and “Clear Keywords After Import” workstation options are enabled the Import dialog refreshes after an import and clears the default keyword values assigned to a document type.




If “Do Not Close Dialog After Import” is disabled, after an import is successful the dialog will close, when re-opened the default keyword values will persist where other values will not.

summary: Web Client: Stamp Version Context Menu Option Present on Revisions Prior to Most Recent Stamp.
description:
When viewing a document with multiple revisions, and stamping one of the least most recent revisions yet not oldest, should prevent any prior revisions to that from also being stamped. However, when right clicking a prior revision, the Stamp Version option should not be present. When subsequently choosing the Stamp Version option, the Stamp Version dialog appears, allows for text to be entered into the Version Name field, but clicking ‘OK’ does not yield any action. This generates an an InvalidOperationException within Diagnostics Console.




Found in web client (Firefox) regression testing, sequence #4: onbase://wv/object/?classid=12260&objectid=45117015&Sig=aAC2agovgjDB_aMzH2L-OL3iJWE3y7Ryyyui8FLsAp4~




additionally found here: onbase://wv/object/?classid=12260&objectid=45116766&Sig=-535CrrdzeIxyENz75Dx9uSTAE2tzxDOKjGHoKI520E~

summary: Web Client: Users Without Privilege to Create Revisions can Re-Index a Document as a Revision of Another Document
description: Users without the Create Revisions privilege are able to re-index a document as a revision when the destination doctype has Create Revisions Assume enabled. Found during web client (Firefox) regression testing, sequence #6: onbase://wv/object/?classid=12260&objectid=45116716&Sig=_RqI4k_9NwDwBAAHpBuecnXsYK2ZpGE_O2sppRXd6gg~Test Plan Template ID: 55780Name: Web Client | EDM ServicesTest Template ID: 265145

summary: Exception has been thrown message in Document Separation
description: When a restricted keywords user attempts to split a document with a MIKG on the source document into a new Document Type with the same keywords in a hidden MIKG the client will throw an "Exception has been thrown by the target of an invocation" dialog.This does not occur in Re-Index, there we receive an expected "Unable to re-index to this document type due to a keywords type configuration conflict." dialog.This does not occur when doing loose to hidden loose or SIKG to hidden SIKG.

summary: ChecksumCreator RemoveURL does not remove https urls from the querystring parameter
description: In the ChecksumCreator utility in Hyland.Applications.Web.Security, the constructor strips all but the acutal querystring from the provided querystring parameter. In the RemoveURL method, we are treating `http` and `https` urls differently as the Regex only accounts for the `http` case.As this is feature to help with misconfiguration, we probably don't have many that have relied on this feature, from external use, and internal use within the Web Server, we always only provide the querystring, without the additional url components.

summary: Unity: When importing multiple image files as a multipage image in ad hoc import, processing file name keywords only display the first file's name in the kwp, but all file names are added as processing file name keywords upon import
description: In Unity, ad hoc import allows you to upload multiple image files as an image. When there is a loose processing file name keyword (PFNK), the filename of the first image is added as the value for the PFNK. Subsequently added files do not have their filenames added as PFNK instances.When the file is uploaded, however, instances of the PFNK are added for each file.What the keyword panel is displaying before save is inconsistent with the state of the keywords that are saved. Work with the Keywords PO to determine whether the additional filenames should be displayed in the kwp before save or whether the subsequent filenames should not be added as keywords on save.Whatever logic changes are made, ensure that read-only PFNKs still behave correctly for restricted users.

summary: EP5 Unity: Upload contexts which inherit keywords are allowing restricted users to clear read-only keywords
description: This is affecting contexts such as Document Separation and EDM Checkout. The issue lies in Upload.xaml.cs and can be resolved by backporting CSFMD-1352.

summary: Unity - EDM Checkout: Server-side restricted keyword validation fails if the user changes the doc type in the Save as New Document keyword panel
description: When a document has persistent checkout enabled and the user chooses Save as New Document on check-in, Upload.xaml.cs is going to send its SorceDocumentID up as the doc ID to EndUploadDocument. The issue is that if the user changes the doc type, the keywords aren't preserved. Clearing read-only keywords is a privileged action restricted users are prevented from taking in this kwp, the Clear button does not clear read-only keywords. The panel not preserving keywords on doc type change would allow the user to circumvent this by switching doc types then switching back. Currently, restricted users are prevented from doing this because of a bug: Users who switch doc types in this context are unable to save their changes due to server-side restricted keyword validation.Usually, if we allow users to drop inherited keywords, we set the SourceDocumentID to -1 and reset the keywordHashId/keywordGuid. However, since Upload is always going to send its SourceDocumentID up as the doc ID instead of -1, we can't do this in this context without changing Upload's SourceDocumentID and wiping out the behavior relying on that ID being the source document's on Upload. The proposed change is to preserve keywords on doc type change in this context.Since this would be a change in behavior for the keyword panel, the PO's approval must be obtained before implementing that change.The OLEViewer is in a similar state for the same reason. Check with the PO if that issue should also be resolved on this card or if another card should be created (I don't believe Metadata owns the OLEViewer).

summary: An untranslated message string found when re-index a separate document
description: An untranslated message string found when re-index a separate document as a user who has neither Separate nor Re-index privileges to the source document typeThis message string could not be found in LRM

summary: Spaces missing from warning message when a user in Doc Sep changes a document's document type in a way that causes restricted keyword security degradation
description:
With the restricted keywords doc type override enabled, when a user changes a document's document type in Document Separation a message is output to the Warnings tab of Diagnostic Console if the doc type change resulted in a keyword type having lowered security. This message is missing two spaces.Example of what is being output currently:


"An action was taken on a document which inherits keywords from Document 8024 that may expose Restricted Keywords to unauthorized user(s).Keyword type restriction levels between MF - A10 Hidden Default and MF - A10 Read-Onlydo not match."


How that message should appear:


"An action was taken on a document which inherits keywords from Document 8024 that may expose Restricted Keywords to unauthorized user(s). Keyword type restriction levels between MF - A10 Hidden Default and MF - A10 Read-Only do not match."

summary: Unity: Autofills are not properly handling security masked keywords
Unity: Unable to import document with reestablished connection after user's network connection was lost during import
description: When a document is being uploaded and the Unity Client loses its network connection during the upload process, the document should be able to be successfully uploaded after the connection is reestablished.However, currently the upload with the reestablished connection fails. The Unity Client goes through the proper process for upload, whether it be a first-time import of the document or a revision to an already existing document, and begins the upload process. It displays the progress bar for the file upload, and once it is done, an error pops up with the message "There was a problem while attempting to upload your document. Please contact your administrator." The document cannot be uploaded, no matter how many times 'Import' is selected. To import the document, the document must be cancelled or removed, then re-selected for import. Found during EP5 Regression Testing. All Tests in the Test Case failed with the same error:Test Case #68733 - 'Drop Connection' : onbase://wv/object/?classid=12260&objectid=38368453&Sig=8aZdHQ46738IQ_Uqx-fxrlFGrPL9ix9CDFeZomJwMRY~

summary: Merging an existing value into a masked keyword dataset results in an ODBC error
description:
When an end user attempts to merge an existing value into a dataset for a masked keyword an ODBC error is displayed. Giving them the option to Abort, Retry or Ignore.


Aborting will close out of the client, retrying will constantly prompt the same ODBC error, ignoring will add a 2nd instance of the masked keyword to the dataset. After re-opening the dialog the 2nd instance is removed.This was found in 21.1.4.23 and recreated in 22.1.0.99The ODBC Error will occur when importing values from the text box in the dialog, or when selecting import from file.Occurs when using ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server, ODBC Driver 13 for SQL Server, SQL Server Native Client 11.0

summary: Safari Upload Document giving Timeout Exception Window During Long-Running File Uploads
description:
Users using latest version of Safari cannot complete upload document process without hitting a timeout exception. The timeout occurs when the upload takes over 10 seconds to complete, and is easiest to reproduce when using a smaller document size over a high latency connection. Using larger documents to reproduce the issue can cause the Web Client to use a "chunking" behavior for upload, splitting the upload into smaller pieces and completing it over multiple requests. This can, counter-intuitively, bypass the timeout issue.Safari has a built-in limitation for synchronous XHRs, that will cause them to timeout after 10 seconds. There does not appear to be a way to customize this behavior.See: 
https://shmeeps.org/2014/05/25/safari-timeout-issue/See also: 
https://stackoverflow.com/a/22767157This built-in timeout causes the request to fail on the front-end, but the server is able to successfully complete the upload. However, we should research if the entire file date is obtained by the server, or if the file is corrupted because the upload is "cut-off".

summary: Web Client | IDOLToolbar.js receives an error when attempting to "View Original Document" for documents that do not display as images
description:
A script error occurs in certain situations within IDOLToolbar.js when attempting to "View Original Document". The issue appears to occur, at least in OPW, due to the inflexible way that IDOLToolbar.js calls the 
displayDocument
 method via 
window.parent
.There is also most likely a similar issue with display original document in new window as well, because that functionality also uses the inflexible method of referencing 
WIndowsManagerA potential solution would be to use the [{$OB.utils.resolve}} method so that the dependency can be obtained irrespective of which parent window in the tree contains the function.

summary: Unity Client: When keywords are applied from a reverse autofill keyset with the Lock Applied Keywords setting enabled the keywords are not being locked
description: Unity Client: When keywords are applied from a reverse autofill keyset with the Lock Applied Keywords setting enabled the keywords are not being locked. Filling from the same RAFKS does lock the keywords in the Thick and Web clients, and the autofill MRG doesn't make any mention of Unity being an exception.

summary: MIKG Encrypted Keywords cause pseudo-error message in Web Client
description: When a MIKG with multiple instances contains an encrypted keyword, a message box will show up for every instance after the first. It seems like the field is complaining about the mask characters. Images attached.This was found in 21.1 during regression and confirmed in 18, so I included the in-between versions as effected.

description: Working in a Serbian-Serbia locale is not possible to index a document, retrieve by dates or perform Full Page OCR.

description:
copied from

summary: Encrypted Security Mask Keywords are not behaving properly
description: Found while writing smoke tests, testing keyword types configured for encryption, security mask, and regular keyword type masking.  For users that are not privileged for unmasking security mask keywords none of the following applies, the integrity of the keyword is being protected by the KeywordHashCache logic treating the masked value as a read-only keyword.  For a privileged user the following scenarios are occurring that should be addressed.1.  The API is using a strategy of defaulting of sending keyword values masked, making the decision to force the user to request the user to unmask the values in order to view or update them which triggers transaction logging for the action.  The desired state would be that the keyword guid generation method gave the option to treat all encrypted keywords as read-only with the masked value so that the API could enable the behavior of generating the keyword guid that respects this strategy.  By default the guid would restrict modification of an encrypted security mask keyword, when the user requests to unmask the value and has rights we would generate a guid that allows them to modify the value and send the unmasked value to the client.2.  Using the combination of Security Mask Keywords and Keyword Type Masking jointly is currently being enforced by the clients not allowing the user to enter in characters that don't match the masking rules.  However, for an API consumer, there is nothing to prevent a user from sending up values that do not match the mask.  In this case the keyword type masking is not being respected.  The current Core logic in KeywordValueString.cs is overriding the mask using a "ConvertToLenientMaskedString" method that is converting all masking characters to 'X' which represents any valid alphanumeric value which basically means the only thing being enforced is the length of the string, not the contents. A Keyword Type configured with the Keyword Type Mask '000S00S0000' and Security Mask 'MMMAMMAMMAA' for instance could update a keyword value to '!@#$%^&*()' and pass validation.  This is problematic as this causes issues rendering the keyword values in other OnBase Clients.3.  A Keyword Type configured for only Security Masking can trigger incorrect validation and incorrect storing of keywords in the scenario where a user wants to update a keyword value containing the security mask character.  For instance saving a value configured with 'MMMAMMAMMAA' with a value '123-45-67*9 would cause that keyword value to be ignored and the rest of the keywords being saved without any indication of a error occurring. This behavior is occurring in 'addSecurityMaskedKeywordTypesToList()' in KeywordDifference.cs4.  As a result of these issues and other related issues that have occurred, the API should have the strategy of only accepting normalized values (values that have no masking applied with the correct regional settings applied) for Keyword Modification as a strategy.  The ability for the consumer to tell the server that it will be sending up formatted values could be supported in the future, but expecting the server to accept both at the same time with potential for a mix of some formatted values and some normalized values should not be supported.This card should be broken up into several stories as the work here is mixed between "The Core" and the API backend.

description: Data Validation - normalizeData() function with DateTime is able to parse time part for different locales but for language "vi" a red dotted line is appearing in UI.

summary: Intermittent Issue: “how instructions button collapsed S” is announced/displayed on NVDA screen viewer instead of “Show instructions button collapsed” on Custom Query Screen
description:
On Custom Query screen of web client, after selecting a custom query that has “Show Instructions” button, if TAB ot Shift+TAB is pressed to focus on that button with NVDA switched ON, it should announce the button name as “Show instructions button collapsed“. But if the TAB/Shift+TAB is pressed multiple times to change the focus out of the “Show Instructions” button and back to it, sometimes anomalies have been observed, e.g. instead of showing/announcing 
“Show instructions button collapsed”
, the speech-viewer shows and announces 
“how instructions button collapsed S”
. This phenomenon is observed intermittently, not every time though, on both laptop and VM.

summary: Version name and Version number issue for revised documents in the Web Client
description:
Issue with version name: 
User should not have the option to add a version name when a version name is already added on a revision in the web client.From the revisions/renditions settings if user goes to the Stamp Version for a revision where already a version name has been added, then user is again getting an option to add version name as shown in the picture -If user tries to apply a new version name by typing here, then the below error will come in the console -The new version name is not being updated as typed.Issue with version number:
 For any revision of an image document, if no version has been created then the Version # field shows the version as ‘0’ in the ‘Stamp Revision’ option in the web client. Whereas in Unity Client it is kept as blank as no version has been added for that revision.

summary: Importing data into a Cascading Dataset that leverages a Keyword set to '250' characters is causing the OnBase Configuration Client to close unexpectedly.
description:
[ Template ]


Technical Specialist - Defect RFA [OnBase][ System Type ]


OnBase[ Module / Product Line Functionality ]


Configuration[ Summary / Description ]


Importing data into a Cascading Dataset that leverages a Keyword set to '250' characters is causing the OnBase Configuration Client to close unexpectedly.[ Business Impact ]


Admin is unable to finish making the dataset for the users due to this behavior.[ Background ]


Error that is thrown in Event Viewer:


Faulting application name: obcfg32.exe, version: 23.1.0.82, time stamp: 0x62f92a4d


Faulting module name: ucrtbase.dll, version: 10.0.19041.789, time stamp: 0x82dc99a2


Exception code: 0xc0000409


Fault offset: 0x0009d132


Faulting process id: 0x539c


Faulting application start time: 0x01d8b639b8fac588


Faulting application path: C:\Users\KJanka\Desktop\OnBase Builds\EP7\23.1.0.82 Thick Client\obcfg32.exe


Faulting module path: C:\WINDOWS\System32\ucrtbase.dll


Report Id: 62cede0c-cb86-4a9e-b427-48e0122af159


Faulting package full name:


Faulting package-relative application ID: [ Actual Behavior vs. Desired Behavior ]


Cascading Datasets that leverage an import file of 250 characters causing OnBase Configuration Client to close unexpectedly. Desired behavior would be to allow this import to occur considering the keyword being leveraged is set to allow 250 characters.[ Customer Pain ]


2[ Defect Classification ]


Usability[ Related Version/Build ]

Yes[ Location Replicated ]

summary: Unity: Document purged toast appears when attempting to re-index a document into a doctype with revisions enabled if "save as revision" is selected
description:
During document re-index, if the document's new doctype supports revisions/renditions (with prompt) and the document has keywords that are identical to a document already in the new doctype, the user will be prompted to decide how they want to save the new document.


If the user opts to save the document as a revision, but does not have Document Maintenance privileges, the revision will be added to the target document, while the original document will not be removed, but a success toast message will appear stating the original document had been removed.

summary: Harmony - Keyword panel throws an exception for keywords that use masking
description: It seems like the ob-kwp-index-keyword-panel component throws an error when loading keywords that contain masking. The error says:ExpressionChangedAfterItHasBeenCheckedError: Expression has changed after it was checked. Previous value: 'Social Security Number : 125747895'. Current value: 'Social Security Number : 125-74-7895'.An example of this can be seen in harmony when using the Quick Access app and loading a document with the ID 5799

summary: [Web Client] AFKS - Pressing F6 or TAB While Placing Cursor On The Primary Keyword Field After Deleting Other Or Primary Keyword Field Doesn't Auto-Populate Keyword Fields
description: While importing a document in AFKS configured document type, if the primary key is entered and F6/TAB is pressed, the other keyword values get auto-populated for the 1st time correctly. Then, if any of the keyword field values are deleted using backspace key, and then F6/TAB is pressed after placing the cursor on the Primary Keyword field, the other keyword fields don’t get auto-populated anymore. Workaround: If the Clear Keyword button is pressed, and then the above steps are followed, the keyword fields get auto-populated as expected.Exception: For MIKG instances, this discrepancy is not observed.

summary: Encypted Security Mask Keywords not behaving properly
description:
Copied directly from 
https://jira.hylandqa.net/browse/CSFM-1934Found while writing smoke tests, testing keyword types configured for encryption, security mask, and regular keyword type masking.  For users that are not privileged for unmasking security mask keywords none of the following applies, the integrity of the keyword is being protected by the KeywordHashCache logic treating the masked value as a read-only keyword.  For a privileged user the following scenarios are occurring that should be addressed.1.  The API is using a strategy of defaulting of sending keyword values masked, making the decision to force the user to request the user to unmask the values in order to view or update them which triggers transaction logging for the action.  The desired state would be that the keyword guid generation method gave the option to treat all encrypted keywords as read-only with the masked value so that the API could enable the behavior of generating the keyword guid that respects this strategy.  By default the guid would restrict modification of an encrypted security mask keyword, when the user requests to unmask the value and has rights we would generate a guid that allows them to modify the value and send the unmasked value to the client.2.  Using the combination of Security Mask Keywords and Keyword Type Masking jointly is currently being enforced by the clients not allowing the user to enter in characters that don't match the masking rules.  However, for an API consumer, there is nothing to prevent a user from sending up values that do not match the mask.  In this case the keyword type masking is not being respected.  The current Core logic in KeywordValueString.cs is overriding the mask using a "ConvertToLenientMaskedString" method that is converting all masking characters to 'X' which represents any valid alphanumeric value which basically means the only thing being enforced is the length of the string, not the contents. A Keyword Type configured with the Keyword Type Mask '000S00S0000' and Security Mask 'MMMAMMAMMAA' for instance could update a keyword value to '!@#$%^&*()' and pass validation.  This is problematic as this causes issues rendering the keyword values in other OnBase Clients.3.  A Keyword Type configured for only Security Masking can trigger incorrect validation and incorrect storing of keywords in the scenario where a user wants to update a keyword value containing the security mask character.  For instance saving a value configured with 'MMMAMMAMMAA' with a value '123-45-67*9 would cause that keyword value to be ignored and the rest of the keywords being saved without any indication of a error occurring. This behavior is occurring in 'addSecurityMaskedKeywordTypesToList()' in KeywordDifference.cs4.  As a result of these issues and other related issues that have occurred, the API should have the strategy of only accepting normalized values (values that have no masking applied with the correct regional settings applied) for Keyword Modification as a strategy.  The ability for the consumer to tell the server that it will be sending up formatted values could be supported in the future, but expecting the server to accept both at the same time with potential for a mix of some formatted values and some normalized values should not be supported. This card should be broken up into several stories as the work here is mixed between "The Core" and the API backend.
